message:
security(handoff): prevent path traversal and symlink attacks in writer, add modtime fallback in reader

Harden the handoff read/write system against filesystem-based attacks
and edge cases:

**Symlink traversal prevention (writer.go):**
Four new helpers — ensureNoSymlinkComponents, ensureSafeDir,
validateManagedHandoffPath, normalizeWriterSessionName — enforce that
no path component is a symlink when creating directories, writing,
archiving, or deleting handoff files. All directory creation calls now
go through ensureSafeDir instead of bare os.MkdirAll.

**Path validation consolidation (writer.go):**
Delete and Archive previously had ad-hoc "is path within base dir?"
string prefix checks. Both refactored to use unified
validateManagedHandoffPath which also enforces symlink safety and
ensures the file is a regular .yaml file.

**Hidden directory/file filtering (reader.go, writer.go):**
FindLatestAny in the reader now skips directories prefixed with dot.
Rotation logic in the writer uses new visibleYAMLPaths helper to
exclude hidden/symlinked YAML files from rotation counts.

**Modtime fallback for missing created_at (reader.go):**
When FindLatestAny encounters a handoff with zero CreatedAt, it falls
back to the file's OS modification time for comparison, preventing a
zero-time from always losing to any real handoff.

**Tests (reader_test.go, writer_test.go):**
Comprehensive coverage for modtime fallback, hidden directory skipping,
symlink rejection on EnsureDir/Write/Archive/Delete, and rotation
skipping symlinked YAML files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
